title: Allantodia amamiana

This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, diarrhea, and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Allantodia amamiana is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and it is also used in the production of extracts and capsules, which are sold as dietary supplements.
taxonomy: Allantodia amamiana is a species of fern in the family Woodsiaceae. It is classified as a member of the genus Allantodia, which is a group of ferns commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The family Woodsiaceae is a group of ferns that are typically characterized by their simple leaves and absence of sporangia on the leaves.
